summary: Early American painter. Known for The Artist in His Museum, Exhumation of the Mastodon, The Staircase Group, and portraits of George Washington. Named his sons after famous painters (Raphaelle, Rembrandt, Titian, Rubens, Linnaeus, Franklin). Exchanged a saddle for art lessons from John Hesselius.

works.name: The Artist in His Museum (1822)
works.description: Self-portrait lifting a red curtain to reveal a vast gallery of taxidermied objects and stuffed birds.

works.name: Exhumation of the Mastodon (1806)
works.description: Shows a pulley system going into a pit at Barber farm; sons Linnaeus, Franklin, Titian, and Alexander Wilson present.

comprehensive_summary: Charles Willson Peale was an early American painter who named his sons after famous artists — Raphaelle, Rembrandt, Titian, Rubens, Linnaeus, and Franklin. His self-portrait The Artist in His Museum shows him lifting a red curtain to reveal a gallery of taxidermied objects. Exhumation of the Mastodon depicts a pulley system at Barber farm with his sons and Alexander Wilson present. The Staircase Group is a trompe l'oeil showing two of his sons. He painted many portraits of George Washington including George Washington at Princeton. He exchanged a saddle for art lessons from John Hesselius. Rachel Weeping shows a deceased child. He named a daughter Sophonisba after Sofonisba Anguissola. His slave Moses Williams became a silhouette maker.
